function.name: get_top_candidates
function.description:
Get the top pre-scored pool candidates for deployment review.
All filtering, scoring, and rule-checking is done in code — no analysis needed.
Returns the top N eligible pools ranked by score (fee/TVL, organic, stability, volume).
Each pool includes a score (0-100) and has already passed all hard disqualifiers.
Use this instead of discover_pools for screening cycles.
If this returns one candidate, still judge whether it is actually worth deploying; one weak candidate should be skipped.

limit.description: Number of top candidates to return. Default 3.

function.name: get_momentum_candidates
function.description:
Spot-trade candidate source for Hunter (Trader-Screener).
Unlike get_top_candidates (which is tuned for DLMM fee yield), this surfaces tokens with POSITIVE 5m price momentum but NOT in blow-off territory.
Filters out: tokens with no 5m price change (no momentum), tokens with >50% 5m spike (likely peak/distribution), pools below TVL/volume floor, occupied tokens, cooldowns.
Sorts by momentumScore = min(priceChange, 25) × log(1 + turnover).
Use this as Hunter's primary candidate source instead of get_top_candidates.

min_price_change.description: Minimum 5m price change %. Default 3. Raise for stronger momentum, lower for early entries.

max_price_change.description: Maximum 5m price change %. Default 50. Pools above this are likely blow-off / distribution.

min_tvl.description: Minimum pool TVL. Default 20000. Sets exit-liquidity floor.

min_volume.description: Minimum pool volume in the 5m window. Default 5000.

function.name: get_pool_detail
function.description:
Get detailed info for a specific DLMM pool by address.
Use this during management to check current pool health (volume, fees, organic score, price trend).
Default timeframe is 5m for real-time accuracy during position management.
Use a longer timeframe (1h, 4h) only when screening for new deployments.

IMPORTANT: Only call this with a real pool address from get_my_positions or get_top_candidates. Never guess or construct a pool address.

pool_address.description: The on-chain pool address (base58 public key)

timeframe.description: Data timeframe. Default 5m for management (most accurate). Use 4h+ for screening.

function.name: get_active_bin
function.description:
Get the current active bin and price for a DLMM pool.
This is an on-chain call via the SDK. Returns:
- binId: the current active bin number
- price: human-readable price (token X per token Y)
- pricePerLamport: raw price in lamports

Only call this if you need the current price to calculate a specific bin range (e.g. user requested a % range). Do NOT call before every deploy — deploy_position fetches the active bin internally.

function.name: deploy_position
function.description:
Open a new DLMM liquidity position.

PRIORITY ORDER for strategy and bins:
1. User explicitly specifies → always follow exactly (user override is absolute)
2. No user spec → use active strategy's lp_strategy and choose bins based on volatility

HARD RULES:
- Never use 'curve'.
- Bin Step: Only deploy in pools with bin_step between 80 and 125.
- Volatility must be positive. If volatility is 0, null, or missing, do not deploy.
- Range must cover at least 35 total bins. Never deploy 1-bin/tiny ranges.
- For single-side SOL deploys (amount_y only, amount_x=0), do not request upside exposure:
  use bins_below only, keep bins_above=0, and the upper bin will be pinned to the current active bin.

Guidelines (only when user hasn't specified):
- Strategy: use the active strategy's lp_strategy field (bid_ask or spot)
- Bins: choose from configured minBinsBelow/maxBinsBelow by positive volatility. The hard lower floor is 35 bins.
- Deposit: single-sided SOL only: set amount_y/amount_sol, keep amount_x=0.

WARNING: This executes a real on-chain transaction. Check DRY_RUN mode.

pool_address.description: The DLMM pool address to LP in

amount_y.description: Amount of quote token (usually SOL) to deposit.

amount_x.description: Unsupported for this agent. Keep at 0; deploys are single-side SOL via amount_y.

amount_sol.description: Alias for amount_y. For backward compatibility.

strategy.description: DLMM strategy type. If user specifies, use exactly what they said. Otherwise use the active strategy's lp_strategy field.

bins_below.description: Number of bins below the current active bin. For single-side SOL deploys, this is the main range input: lower bin = active bin - bins_below, upper bin = active bin.

bins_above.description: Number of bins above the current active bin. Keep this at 0 for single-side SOL deploys. Only use this for dual-sided or explicit upside-exposure deploys.

downside_pct.description: Optional human-friendly downside range in percent below the current active price. Converted to bins internally via the Meteora SDK.

upside_pct.description: Optional human-friendly upside range in percent above the current active price. Do not use this for single-side SOL deploys.

base_mint.description: Base token mint address — used to prevent duplicate token exposure across pools

volatility.description: Pool volatility at deploy time, sourced from max(screening timeframe, 30m)

organic_score.description: Base token organic score at deploy time

confidence.description: Argus PROCEED confidence (0..1). Executor scales position: 0.85=50%, 0.95+=100% of computeDeployAmount. Always pass the confidence from the verdict you received.

function.name: get_position_pnl
function.description:
Get detailed PnL and real-time Fee/TVL metrics for an open position.
Use this during management to check if yield has dropped significantly.
Returns current feePerTvl24h which indicates the current APY of the pool.

function.name: get_my_positions
function.description:
List all open DLMM positions for the agent wallet.
Returns positions grouped by pool, each with:
- position address
- pool address and token pair
- bin range (min/max bin IDs)
- whether currently in range
- unclaimed fees (in USD)
- total deposited value vs current value
- time since last rebalance

Use this at the start of every management cycle.

function.name: claim_fees
function.description:
Claim accumulated swap fees from a specific position.
Only call when unclaimed fees > $5 to justify transaction costs.
Returns the transaction hash and amounts claimed.

WARNING: This executes a real on-chain transaction.

position_address.description: The position public key to claim fees from

function.name: close_position
function.description:
Remove all liquidity and close a position.
This withdraws all tokens back to the wallet and closes the position account.
Use when:
- Position has been out of range for > 30 minutes
- IL exceeds accumulated fees
- Token shows danger signals (organic score drop, volume crash)
- Rebalancing (close old + open new)

WARNING: This executes a real on-chain transaction. Cannot be undone.

position_address.description: The position public key to close

skip_swap.description: Set to true if user explicitly wants to hold/keep the base token after closing. Default: false (auto-swaps base token back to SOL).

reason.description: Why this position is being closed. Include the rule that triggered it, e.g. 'low yield', 'stop loss', 'trailing TP', 'OOR'. Used for pool memory.

function.name: get_wallet_balance
function.description:
Get current wallet balances for SOL, USDC, and all other token holdings.
Returns:
- SOL balance (native)
- USDC balance
- Other SPL token balances with USD values
- Total portfolio value in USD

Use to check available capital before deploying positions.

function.name: swap_token
function.description:
Swap tokens via Jupiter aggregator.
Use when you need to rebalance wallet holdings, e.g.:
- Convert claimed fee tokens back to SOL/USDC
- Prepare token pair before deploying a position

WARNING: This executes a real on-chain transaction.

input_mint.description: Mint address of the token to sell

output_mint.description: Mint address of the token to buy

amount.description: Amount of input token to swap (in human-readable units, not lamports)

function.name: update_config
function.description:
Update any of your operating parameters at runtime.
Changes persist to user-config.json and take effect immediately — no restart needed.

VALID KEYS (use EXACTLY these key names, nothing else):
Screening: minFeeActiveTvlRatio, minTvl, maxTvl, minVolume, minOrganic, minQuoteOrganic, minHolders, minMcap, maxMcap, minBinStep, maxBinStep, timeframe, category, minTokenFeesSol, excludeHighSupplyConcentration, allowedLaunchpads, blockedLaunchpads
Management: minClaimAmount, outOfRangeBinsToClose, outOfRangeWaitMinutes, oorCooldownTriggerCount, oorCooldownHours, repeatDeployCooldownEnabled, repeatDeployCooldownTriggerCount, repeatDeployCooldownHours, repeatDeployCooldownScope, repeatDeployCooldownMinFeeEarnedPct, minVolumeToRebalance, stopLossPct, takeProfitPct, minSolToOpen, deployAmountSol, gasReserve, positionSizePct
Risk: maxPositions, maxDeployAmount
Schedule: managementIntervalMin, screeningIntervalMin
Models: managementModel, screeningModel, generalModel
Strategy: minBinsBelow, maxBinsBelow, defaultBinsBelow (legacy binsBelow maps to maxBinsBelow)

Reason is optional but helpful — logged as a lesson when provided.

changes.description: Key-value pairs of settings to update. e.g. { "takeProfitPct": 8 }

reason.description: Why you are making this change — what you observed that justified it

function.name: check_smart_wallets_on_pool
function.description:
Check if any tracked smart wallets have an active position in a given pool.
Use this before deploying to gauge confidence — if smart wallets are in the pool it's a strong signal.
If no smart wallets are present, rely on fundamentals (fees, volume, organic score) as usual.

function.name: get_token_info
function.description:
Get token data from Jupiter (organic score, holders, audit, price stats, mcap).
Use this to research a token before deploying or when the user asks about a token.
Accepts token name, symbol, or mint address as query.

Returns: organic score, holder count, mcap, liquidity, audit flags (mint/freeze disabled, bot holders %), 1h and 24h stats.

query.description: Token name, symbol, or mint address

function.name: get_token_holders
function.description:
Get holder distribution for a token by mint address.
Fetches top 100 holders — use limit to control how many to display (default 20).
Each holder includes: address, amount, % of supply, SOL balance, tags (Pool/AMM/etc), and funding info (who funded this wallet, amount, slot).
is_pool=true means it's a liquidity pool address, not a real holder — filter these out when analyzing concentration.

Also returns global_fees_sol — total priority/jito tips paid by ALL traders on this token (NOT Meteora LP fees).
This is a key signal: low global_fees_sol means transactions are bundled or the token is a scam.
HARD GATE: if global_fees_sol < config.screening.minTokenFeesSol (default 30), do NOT deploy.

NOTE: Requires mint address. If you only have a symbol/name, call get_token_info first to resolve the mint.

mint.description: Token mint address (base58). Use get_token_info first if you only have a symbol.

limit.description: How many holders to return (default 20, max 100)

function.name: study_top_lpers
function.description:
Fetch and analyze top open LPers for a pool to learn from their behaviour.
Returns LPAgent-backed owner aggregates and historical style/range samples from
the Agent Meridian `/study-top-lp/:pool` endpoint.

Use this before deploying into a new pool to:
- See if top performers are scalpers (< 1h holds) or long-term holders.
- Match your strategy and range to what is actually working for others right now.
- Avoid pools where even the best open LPs are poorly placed or losing.

Server note: study data is cached and refreshed every 30 minutes.

pool_address.description: Pool address to study top LPers for

limit.description: Number of top LPers to study. Default 4.

function.name: set_position_note
function.description:
Save a persistent instruction for a position that ALL future management cycles will respect.
Use this immediately whenever the user gives a specific instruction about a position:
- "hold until 5% profit"
- "don't close before fees hit $10"
- "close if it goes out of range"
- "hold for at least 2 hours"

The instruction is stored in state.json and injected into every management cycle prompt.
Pass null or empty string to clear an existing instruction.

position_address.description: The position address to attach the instruction to

instruction.description: The instruction to persist (e.g. 'hold until PnL >= 5%'). Pass empty string to clear.

function.name: recenter_position
function.description:
Re-center a position around the CURRENT active bin to keep liquidity earning fees.
It closes the position, swaps any base token back to SOL, and redeploys single-sided SOL around the new active bin (same pool).
Use when a position has drifted off the active bin (price moved) but the pool is still healthy — instead of closing dead.
This is how top LP performers keep fees flowing. Executes real on-chain transactions.

position_address.description: The position public key to re-center

bins_below.description: Optional bins below active bin for the new range; defaults to the configured strategy default.

function.name: get_token_narrative
function.description:
Get the narrative or story behind a token from Jupiter ChainInsight.
Returns a plain-text description of what the token is about — its origin, theme, community, and activity.
Use during token evaluation to understand if there is a real catalyst driving attention and volume.

GOOD narrative signals (proceed with more confidence):
- Specific origin story: tied to a real-world event, viral moment, person, animal, place, or cultural reference
- Active community: mentions contests, donations, real-world actions, organized activities
- Trending catalyst: references something currently viral on X/CT (KOL call, news event, meme wave)
- Named entities: real identifiable subjects (a specific animal, person, project, game, etc.)

BAD narrative signals (caution or skip):
- Empty or null — no story at all
- Pure hype/financial language only: "next 100x", "to the moon", "fair launch gem" with no substance
- Completely generic: "community-driven token", "meme coin" with zero specific context
- Copy-paste of another token's narrative

function.name: search_pools
function.description:
Search for DLMM pools by token symbol, ticker, or contract address (CA).
Use this when the user asks to deploy into a specific token or pool by name/CA,
or when you want to find pools for a specific token outside of the normal screening flow.

Examples: "find pools for ROSIE", "search BONK pools", "look up pool for CA abc123..."

Returns pool address, name, bin_step, fee %, TVL, volume, and token mints.

query.description: Token symbol, ticker name, or contract address to search for

function.name: get_pool_memory
function.description:
Check your deploy history for a pool BEFORE deploying.
Returns all past deploys, PnL, win rate, and any notes you've added.

Call this tool before deploying to any pool — you may have been here before and it didn't work.
Also useful during screening to skip pools with a bad track record.

pool_address.description: The pool address to look up

function.name: evaluator
function.description: Run a propose-only meta-agent that reviews recent decisions, lessons, and performance metrics, then writes parameter-tuning proposals to evaluator-proposals.jsonl. Never auto-applies — humans approve via evaluator_apply. Use for strategic review (typically every 24h, or on-demand after a series of trades).

trigger.description: Optional label for what triggered this review (e.g. 'on_demand', 'scheduled', 'cli'). Default 'cli'.

function.name: evaluator_apply
function.description: Apply a previously-written evaluator proposal (config diffs). Validates against the TUNABLE_KEYS whitelist before mutating user-config.json. Defaults to the latest proposal if no id is provided.

id.description: Specific proposal id to apply. Omit to apply the latest.

function.name: compressor
function.description: Semantically merge a role's accumulated lessons to reduce noise. By default compresses every role whose lesson count exceeds threshold. Safe — never touches pinned or auto-evolution lessons. Backs up pre-compression state.

role.description: Optional. Compress one specific role. Omit to compress all over-threshold roles.

force.description: Bypass the threshold check. Default false.

function.name: get_dex_velocity
function.description: Real-time price + volume velocity for a Meteora pool. Returns DexScreener 5m/1h/6h/24h price/volume/txns AND (when available) an accurate keyless Birdeye overlay in `birdeye_velocity` (30m/1h/2h/4h/24h with buy/sell split + unique wallets + holders). When `velocity_source` is "birdeye", PREFER `birdeye_velocity` — the DexScreener short-windows (esp. 5m) are unreliable for thin pools. Use when verifying a pool just before deploy.

function.name: get_rugcheck_report
function.description: RugCheck.xyz risk report for a token mint. Returns rug score (0-100, lower=better), rugged flag, risk summary (danger/warn counts + names), top holder concentration, and a verdict_hint. Use to verify a token isn't a known scam before deploying. Does not catch every bad token — combine with on-chain signals.

function.name: get_pumpfun_status
function.description: Pump.fun token status: graduated (migrated to AMM) vs pre-graduation (still on bonding curve), creator wallet, age in hours, market cap, socials, and a verdict_hint. Pre-graduation tokens are statistically much higher rug risk — VETO unless smart wallets present.

function.name: get_lp_cohort
function.description: FULL LP cohort PnL distribution for a DLMM pool. Decodes every open position in the pool, groups by owner, fetches PnL via Meteora's API for each unique LP, and aggregates: total_positions, total_lps, profitable_pct, median_pnl_pct, p25/p75, top10_by_size_avg_pnl_pct, and a verdict_hint (STRONG_GREEN / STRONG_RED). Highest-signal pool-quality check — answers 'are most LPs actually making money?' Use during pool review. Slower than other checks (~1-2s), worth the time.

max_owners_sampled.description: Cap on unique LPs to sample (default 60). Lower for faster cycles, higher for more accurate stats on large pools.

function.name: add_pool_note
function.description: Append a freeform note to a poolapos;s memory file. Use this after a position closes or recenters to record observed behavior (e.g. OOR pattern, dump timing, fee/TVL trajectory, final PnL). Future Scout/Argus will read these via get_pool_memory.

note.description: Concise observation (1-2 sentences). Lead with the signal ("went OOR 14 min after deploy"), then a brief WHY if known.

function.name: list_smart_wallets
function.description: Return the current list of tracked smart wallets (alpha KOLs, top LPers) — addresses, names, categories, types.

function.name: add_smart_wallet
function.description: Add a wallet to the tracked smart-wallet list. Use after observing strong realized PnL from this wallet on recent pools we deployed into.

name.description: Short label for the wallet, e.g. 'kol_pmoney', 'alpha_lp_5'.

type.description: 'lp' = LP-side wallet (use for LP cohort checks); 'holder' = token-holding wallet.

function.name: remove_smart_wallet
function.description: Remove a wallet from the tracked list. Use when a previously-smart wallet has been underperforming (e.g. multiple negative-PnL exits over the review window).

function.name: estimate_close_slippage
function.description: Estimate the slippage cost (USD + %) of closing a specific position right now. Internally calls Jupiter quote API to price the base->SOL swap that would execute on close, and compares against position spot value. Returns 'estimated_slippage_pct' (the headline number) and 'interpretation' (HIGH / MODERATE / LOW). Use BEFORE every close: if slippage > 5%, hold or recenter unless pool is dying.

function.name: get_birdeye_velocity
function.description: Accurate keyless Birdeye per-token velocity at 30m/1h/2h/4h/24h: price change %, volume USD (total + buy/sell split), buy/sell counts, trade count, unique wallets (+change%), plus holder count, liquidity, mcap. Works for ANY token mint (not just trending). Higher fidelity than DexScreener short-windows for momentum/trajectory checks. No 5m window exists — 30m is the shortest.

function.name: get_birdeye_security
function.description: Keyless Birdeye token security/risk report: mintable (mint authority), freeze authority, mint renounced, mutable metadata, creator address + %, owner %, top-10 holder %, token-2022 / transfer-fee flags. Use alongside get_rugcheck_report to verify a token isn't a rug before deploying/buying.

function.name: get_birdeye_gems
function.description: Keyless Birdeye 'find-gems' trending token list with per-window (1h/4h/24h) velocity, unique wallets, holder count and top-10 concentration. An ADDITIONAL momentum/trending candidate source beyond Meteora discovery — useful when the primary candidate feed is dry. Returns tokens with mint, symbol, price, mcap, liquidity.

function.name: get_birdeye_ohlcv
function.description: Keyless Birdeye OHLCV candles for a token at ANY resolution (1s/5s/1m/5m/15m/1h/...). Returns {res, candles:[{t,o,h,l,c,v}]} newest last (v = base-token volume; xclose ~= USD). Use to compute precise short-window momentum/volume (e.g. true 5m) or inspect the recent price path. Params: mint (required), res (default 5m), count (default 60).

function.name: get_birdeye_token_stats
function.description: Keyless Birdeye multi-timeframe token stats in ONE call — NATIVE price change at 5m/30m/1h/2h/4h/8h/24h (no OHLCV computation needed), buy/sell counts + dollar-volume split, uniqueTraders, market count, liquidity, mcap, launchpad + pump.fun bonding-curve status. THE multi-timeframe liveness read for spot screening: a quiet 5m with healthy 1h volume + liquidity = ALIVE, not dead. Prefer this over get_birdeye_ohlcv for 5m/8h momentum.

function.name: get_birdeye_holders
function.description: Keyless Birdeye ranked live top holders for a token: owner address, rank, ui_amount, alias (CEX/known wallet). Use to spot whale/dev concentration and live dump risk beyond the static top-10% in get_birdeye_security.

function.name: get_birdeye_markets
function.description: Keyless Birdeye list of ALL markets/pools for a token (name, source/DEX, liquidity, 24h volume) sorted by volume. Reveals the deepest venue and liquidity fragmentation the Meteora-only candidate feed can't see.

function.name: get_gmgn_wallet_tags
function.description: Keyless GMGN wallet-cohort counts for a token — the single best rug/dump filter. Counts of smart / renowned / fresh / sniper / bundler / creator / rat_trader / whale / top / following wallets currently holding. High sniper+bundler+fresh with low smart+renowned = trap; healthy smart+renowned = real interest. Use before proposing a spot buy or LP deploy.

function.name: get_gmgn_top_buyers
function.description: Keyless GMGN early-buyer dump status — of the first ~70 buyers: how many already sold, holding_rate, bought_rate, top10_holder_rate, top70 sniper hold rate, and how many smart-money wallets are positioned. Low holding_rate / high sold = early money already gone (avoid/exit). High holding_rate + smart money in = conviction.

function.name: get_gmgn_pool_fee
function.description: Keyless GMGN per-pool fee config for a token: exchange, liquidity, fee_ratio, is_dynamic_fee, pool_type, and Meteora DAMM v2 / virtual-curve fee configs. DLMM-relevant for Scout when sizing fee yield and picking a venue.

function.name: get_agent_health
function.description: Per-agent health snapshot. Returns {agents:[{agent, enabled, mins_since_active, llm_errors_2h, llm_errors_24h, error_types, broken_tools, verdict}], unhealthy:[...]} where verdict is healthy|idle-ok|recovering|degraded|stalled|dead. Call at the START of every orchestrator tick to detect agents that cannot work — context overflow, broken candidate sources (Unknown command / no data), stalled crons (enabled but idle for hours/days), or repeated LLM errors. idle-ok = disabled by design (no work). recovering = was failing in 24h but quiet now.

function.name: send_alert
function.description: Send a one-line Telegram alert to the human operator. Use ONLY to surface agent-health problems you detect (alert-only policy — do NOT auto-remediate schedules or trigger agents from health findings). Keep it concise.
